text: There are two types of iteration: {{c1::bounded}} and {{c1::unbounded}}. {{c1::Bounded}} is for numerically controlled cycles (for, do), and {{c1::unbounded}} is for logically controlled cycles (while, repeat).
Commands are more in {{c1::imperative}} languages, while expressions are more in {{c1::functional}} languages
All common programming languages using static scope, also use {{c1::deep}} binding

An object is a box that contains:
{{c1::Hidden Data}}: variables, values; even functions.
{{c2::Public operations}}: methods

It is possible to discern types of operation and an “execution method” common to all interpreters. The type of operation executed by the interpreter and assocated data structures, fall into the following categories:
1. {{c1::Operations for processing primitive data}}
2. {{c2::Operations and data structures for controlling the sequence of execution of operations}}
3. {{c3::Operations and data structures for controlling data transfers}}
4. {{c4::Operations and data structures for memory management}}.

The storage component of a physical computer is composed of various levels of memory. Secondary memory implemented using {{c1::optical or magnetic components}}; primary memory, organised as a linear sequence of {{c2::cells, or words}}, of fixed size (usually a multiple of 8 bits); {{c3::cache}} and the {{c4::registers}} which are internal to the CPU
